confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_01cb73bc-deab-44a6-8099-9b92d8aa8f8c read back as received

local_task.title: Implement live OSA activity projection for overlay labels

rationale: The operator needs to see what inhabited agents are doing without opening every terminal or workboard.
Task state alone is insufficient because an OSA can be busy with review, inbox, messaging, or blocked coordination.
Idle is the unit state and should not add label noise.
This projection likely belongs in Narada doctrine/product surface because it generalizes beyond one Windows overlay.
recommendation: Add doctrinal/product language for OSA activity projection as separate from task lifecycle authority.
Define canonical activity-state enum or extensible typed state family.
Clarify which states are operator-visible and which are internal/evidence-only.
Ensure future operator-surface adapters can render this projection consistently.
payload.summary: Narada-andrey discovered that overlay labels should not merely project current task affinity. They should project an Operator Surface Agent activity state machine: idle as hidden/default, with non-idle states such as executing, in_review/awaiting_review, reviewing, blocked, processing inbox, and messaging visible to the operator surface. The local User Site commissioned task 79 to implement this for labels.
payload.title: Define Operator Surface Agent activity projection as first-class state
